title: Lula Rejects US Terrorism Designation for Brazil Drug Gangs
summary: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva blasted a US decision to label two major Brazilian criminal groups as terrorist organizations, calling it a threat to the nation’s sovereignty that won’t help its fight against drug trafficking.

title: MLB Proposes $245 Million Salary Cap That Players Union Rejects
summary:
Major League Baseball proposed a salary cap for the first time in more than three decades, a move opposed by the players union and likely to ignite tension between the two sides ahead of negotiations over a new labor deal.

The league’s proposal would cap team spending at about $245 million and put a salary floor at roughly $171 million, according to a document provided by MLB. The current collective bargaining agreement between the league and players is set to expire in December, leading to speculation there could be a disruption to the 2027 season as the two sides hash out payroll structure.  In 1994, the owners pushed for a salary cap, and the Major League Baseball Players Association went on strike during the second half of the season. The impasse led to a big chunk of games and the World Series being canceled.

Randall Williams, Bloomberg News Business of Sports Reporter, joins Intelligence Radio for a closer look. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Oil Prices Soften as Traders Bet on Renewal of US-Iran Truce
summary: Oil fell as the US and Iran tentatively agreed to extend a ceasefire by 60 days, stoking optimism the Strait of Hormuz may soon reopen.

title: Stocks Rise as Trump Says He’ll Make Call on Iran
summary:
Hopes that a ceasefire deal could pave the way for an end to the Iran conflict drove stocks toward a historic streak of weekly gains, with the market also buoyed by the artificial-intelligence trade.
Brian Belski, CEO and CIO at Humilis Investment Strategies, discusses the S&P 500 bull case and his outlook for markets amid the AI boom. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Rio Tinto’s Aluminum Exports to US Rebound to Pretariff Levels
summary: One of the world’s largest aluminum producers, Rio Tinto Group, says its shipments of the lightweight metal to the US have bounced back to levels from before President Donald Trump’s tariff offensive.

title: Stocks Rise as Trump Says He’ll Make Call on Iran: Markets Wrap
summary: Hopes that a ceasefire deal could pave the way for an end to the Iran conflict drove stocks toward a historic streak of weekly gains, with the market also buoyed by the artificial-intelligence trade.

title: Daniel Boulud Celebrates Le Pavillon 5th Anniversary, Demand for Dining
summary: Daniel Boulud, Chef and Restauranter at Dinex Group, discusses the state of the restaurant industry, food inflation, and upcoming projects including the opening of Brasserie Boulud later this year in New York City. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Stocks Extend Rally on Peace Deal Hopes, AI Spending Optimism
summary: US stocks rose on Friday, boosted by hopes of a peace deal between the US and Iran and expectations that spending on artificial intelligence will drive further gains in corporate earnings.

title: Quarter of Big Oil Tankers Trapped by Iran War Have Escaped
summary: Roughly one-quarter of the non-Iranian large oil tankers trapped inside the Persian Gulf at the outbreak of the Iran war have managed to slip out in a slow, stealthy trickle.

title: Treasuries Set for Best Week Since War Began as Oil Retreats
summary: The US government bond market headed for its best week since the start of the US war on Iran as oil prices declined in anticipation of an agreement to end the conflict.

title: Sugar Climbs Most in a Month on Poor India Monsoon Rain Outlook
summary: Sugar futures in New York posted the biggest gain in a month on concerns that an emerging El Niño weather pattern could curb rainfall and damage the cane crop in India, the world’s second-largest producer.

title: Gold Jumps Most in Three Weeks as Trump Eyes ‘Final’ Iran Call
summary: Gold jumped the most in more than three weeks after US President Donald Trump said he’s making a “final determination” on a preliminary deal to extend a ceasefire with Iran.

title: Iceland’s Own Currency Costs Outweigh Benefits, Ministry Says
summary: The drawbacks to Iceland from having its own currency likely exceed the benefits, the country’s Finance Ministry said, citing the conclusions of a government-commissioned report.

title: Parents Should Start Saving for Child's Education 'at Birth', Says Tricia Scarlata
summary: Tricia Scarlata, Head of Education Savings at JPMorgan Asset Management, discusses college investing, 529 day, and missed opportunities for families saving for college. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Daniel Boulud's COVID-Era Bet
summary: Five years after opening Le Pavillon during the height of COVID, Michelin-starred chef and Dinex Group Owner Daniel Boulud and SL Green CEO Marc Holliday joined Bloomberg Open Interest to talk about how luxury dining helped reinvent New York office real estate. They discuss the return-to-office boom, hospitality-driven workplaces, and why restaurants have become a key tool for attracting talent and premium tenants. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Gap CEO Remains Bullish Despite Lowering Sales Outlook
summary:
Gap Inc. CEO Richard Dickson says the retailer delivered progress in the quarter, even after lowering its sales outlook. He says three out of their four brands are growing and they're raising the earnings outlook. Dickson speaks to Bloomberg's Lisa Abramowicz.

 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Kenya Inflation Heats Up After Iran-Driven Fuel Hikes Fan Unrest
summary: Kenya’s inflation rate rose by the most in more than two years, after higher fuel costs driven by the war in Iran added to price pressures and spurred deadly protests last week.

title: A City-Backed Grocery Store in Atlanta Offers a Model for Mamdani
summary:
Food prices have become the animating issue in one of New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s signature policies: a plan to open city-owned grocery stores.

Azalea Fresh Market's CEO Paul Nair joins Max Chafkin and Stacey Vanek Smith on the Everybody's Business podcast to discuss the popular city-backed grocery store in Atlanta and how it's serving as a model for Mamdani’s grocery store plan.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Paves Way for Onshore Crypto-Linked Perpetual Futures
summary: The main US derivatives regulator said perpetual futures contracts for cryptocurrencies could trade on registered onshore platforms if they meet certain conditions, bringing to America a popular trading instrument that has until now existed almost entirely overseas.

title: Fed’s Bowman Says Too Soon to Judge Iran War Inflation Impact
summary: Federal Reserve Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle Bowman said, “I would like to have more clarity on the economic impacts from the conflict in the Middle East and the durability of those effects,” and thinks it’s too soon to judge the inflationary impact from the Iran war. Bowman said Friday at a central banking conference in Iceland that that policymakers need to look through temporary price shocks. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: BlackRock Scales Back Equities After ‘Generational’ Earnings
summary: BlackRock Inc. is trimming its bet on stocks across its $220 billion model-portfolio business as US equities surge to record highs following a strong earnings season.

title: EU’s Economic Powers Strike Deal to Push Capital Markets Merger
summary: The European Union’s six largest economies have agreed on a plan to implement a bloc-wide capital markets union, reaching a collective stance they say will help spur the continent to action after years of delay.

title: Petra Begins Finsch Mine Rescue Plan Amid Diamond Market Slump
summary: Petra Diamonds Ltd. is putting one of its two mines into a business restructuring process and is considering job cuts across the rest of the company, as falling diamond prices and a strong rand erode earnings and liquidity.

title: Dollar’s Monthly Rise Leaves Strategists Wary of More Gains
summary: This month’s rally in the dollar, as traders priced in the prospect of higher US interest rates, is leaving Wall Street strategists wary of further gains.

title: Hershey Bull Sees World Cup, America 250 Lifting Sales
summary: Summer events including the FIFA World Cup and America’s 250th anniversary could spur sales of sweet treats, making now a key time to snap up beaten-down shares of Hershey Co., according to the candy and snack maker’s newest Wall Street bull.

title: Zurich Bank Aims at Brazil, Florida to Expand in Americas
summary: EFG International AG, a private-banking boutique based in Zurich, is expanding its 300-person team serving clients from the Americas with an emphasis on Brazil and South Florida.

title: Ford Stock Set for Best Month Since Financial Crisis on AI Rally
summary: Ford Motor Co. shares are set to record their best monthly gain in 17 years as the automaker’s newfound status as a possible beneficiary of the artificial intelligence boom sparked an investor frenzy.

title: HSBC’s Kettner Says Blowout Earnings Dim ‘Danger Zone’ Fears
summary: Robust first-quarter corporate results, driven by technology megacaps, have provided fundamental support to the stock-market rally, dimming worries about elevated Treasury yields, according to HSBC Bank Plc’s Max Kettner.

title: Zambia Bonds Jump After 2053 Debt Buyback Tender Starts
summary: Zambia started a cash tender offer to buy back its $1.36 billion bond due in 2053, becoming the latest African nation to repurchase outstanding debt as governments seek to reduce their servicing costs.
